![Marmidon company logo](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

**MARMIDON ENTERPRISE SOFTWARE · WEBSITE PROGRAMME**

**MARM-WEB-CPM-005**

**Content Plan & Page-Content Matrix**

What Goes on Every Page, in What Voice, with What SEO

_The content blueprint for <www.marmidon.com> - the tone of voice, the content blocks for every page template, the per-page SEO metadata, the call-to-action matrix and the content-readiness tracker that turns the structure into a finished, copy-complete website._

| **Document**      | Content Plan & Page-Content Matrix (CPM) |
| ----------------- | ---------------------------------------- |
| **Programme**     | Marmidon Corporate Website Revamp 2026   |
| **Parent**        | MARM-WEB-PRD-001 (Requirements)          |
| **Companions**    | WDD-002, SIA-003, TSR-004                |
| **Version**       | 1.0 - Issued for Development             |
| **Pages covered** | 11 modules · 12 sectors · ~46 templates  |
| **Audience**      | Content team, web developer, SEO         |

**PREPARED BY**

**Marmidon Website Programme - Business Analysis Workstream**

Senior Business Analyst (Corporate & Enterprise Web)

_CONFIDENTIAL - For the Marmidon website development team and authorised stakeholders only._

**Table of Contents**

**SECTION 1**

**Purpose & How to Use**

Turning structure into finished pages

## **1.1 Purpose**

_This document tells the content team and the developer exactly what content each page needs, in what order, in what voice, and with what SEO metadata - so no page ships half-written or off-brand._

The Site Map (SIA-003) defines which pages exist. The Design Document (WDD-002) defines how each template looks. This document defines what goes inside each one. Read together, the three remove ambiguity: a developer can place blocks from the WDD, fill them with the content blocks specified here, and tag each page with the SEO metadata in §8.

**DEVELOPER GUIDANCE · How the developer should use this document**

For each page, build the WDD template, then drop in the content blocks listed here in order.

Use the per-page SEO table (§8) to set the title, meta description and H1.

Use the readiness tracker (§9) to see which pages have final copy and which use approved placeholders for launch.

## **1.2 Content Status Key**

| **Status**      | **Meaning**                                                   |
| --------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **FINAL**       | Copy approved and ready to publish                            |
| **DRAFT**       | Copy written, awaiting Marmidon review                        |
| **PLACEHOLDER** | Approved interim copy; replace before or shortly after launch |
| **TO WRITE**    | Not yet started                                               |

**SECTION 2**

**Tone of Voice & Writing Principles**

How Marmidon sounds

## **2.1 Brand Voice**

_Marmidon sounds like a trusted enterprise software partner: clear, confident, practical and credible - never hype, never jargon for its own sake._

| **Marmidon is…**             | **Marmidon is not…**        |
| ---------------------------- | --------------------------- |
| Clear and plain-spoken       | Buzzword-heavy or vague     |
| Confident and credible       | Boastful or over-promising  |
| Practical and outcome-led    | Abstract or feature-dumping |
| Helpful and consultative     | Pushy or salesy             |
| Enterprise-grade and precise | Casual or sloppy            |

## **2.2 Writing Principles**

- Lead with the outcome, not the feature - say what the customer gains, then how.
- Write for a busy decision-maker: short sentences, scannable structure, meaningful headings.
- Use British English spelling and conventions throughout.
- One clear primary action per page - usually Request a Demo.
- Be specific: name modules, sectors and real outcomes; avoid empty adjectives.
- Every page answers: what is this, who is it for, why Marmidon, what do I do next.

**BEST PRACTICE · The four questions every page must answer**

What is this? · Who is it for? · Why Marmidon? · What do I do next?

If a page does not answer all four above the fold or shortly below, it is not finished.

## **2.3 Terminology (use consistently)**

| **Use this**                 | **Not this**                                           |
| ---------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------ |
| Marmidon Enterprise Software | Marmidon ERP / the system / the software (in headings) |
| Module                       | App / tool / feature set                               |
| Sector / industry            | Vertical / niche                                       |
| Request a Demo               | Book now / Buy / Sign up / Free trial                  |
| Customers                    | Clients / users (on nav)                               |

**SECTION 3**

**Global & Reusable Content Blocks**

These blocks appear on many pages. Write each once, reuse everywhere, so messaging stays uniform (a core requirement of the brief).

| **Block**           | **Content**                                               | **Appears on**                   |
| ------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------- |
| Global header CTA   | "Request a Demo" button, persistent                       | Every page                       |
| Value proposition   | One-sentence statement of what Marmidon does and for whom | Home, About, hubs                |
| Module teaser card  | Icon, module name, one-line benefit, link                 | Home, Products hub, sector pages |
| Sector teaser card  | Sector name, one-line fit statement, link                 | Home, Solutions hub              |
| Proof strip         | Logos / metrics / one-line credibility                    | Home, hubs, sector pages         |
| Testimonial / quote | Customer quote, name, role, company, sector               | Home, case studies, sector pages |
| Final CTA band      | Headline + "Request a Demo" + secondary "Contact us"      | Bottom of nearly every page      |
| Mega-footer         | Module links, sector links, company, legal, contact       | Every page                       |

**MUST-HAVE · Write the value proposition first**

One clear sentence - what Marmidon Enterprise Software is, who it serves, and the outcome it delivers - anchors every other page.

Suggested shape: "Marmidon Enterprise Software is an integrated ERP that runs \[finance, people, stock, sales and operations\] for \[the 12 sectors\], on one platform." Refine the bracketed parts with Marmidon.

**SECTION 4**

**Homepage Content Plan**

/ · template: home

The homepage is the most-visited page and the primary first impression. It must orient any of the five personas (PRD §5) within seconds and route them to the right module, sector or to a demo.

### **Content blocks, in order**

| **#** | **Block**             | **Content to provide**                                                                                | **Status** |
| ----- | --------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------- |
| 1     | Hero                  | Headline (value prop), supporting line, primary CTA (Request a Demo), secondary CTA (Explore modules) | TO WRITE   |
| 2     | Proof strip           | Customer logos or key metrics (e.g. sectors served, modules)                                          | TO WRITE   |
| 3     | What is Marmidon      | 2-3 sentence plain-English explanation + supporting visual                                            | TO WRITE   |
| 4     | Modules overview      | Grid of 11 module teaser cards linking to module pages                                                | TO WRITE   |
| 5     | Solutions by industry | Grid of 12 sector teaser cards linking to sector pages                                                | TO WRITE   |
| 6     | Why Marmidon          | 3-4 differentiators (integrated, sector-fit, proven, supported)                                       | TO WRITE   |
| 7     | Featured proof        | One headline case study or testimonial                                                                | TO WRITE   |
| 8     | Resources teaser      | 2-3 latest articles/guides                                                                            | TO WRITE   |
| 9     | Final CTA band        | Strong demo invitation + contact                                                                      | TO WRITE   |

**DEVELOPER GUIDANCE · Homepage copy guidance**

The hero headline must state the outcome, not "Welcome to Marmidon".

Keep the homepage skimmable - it is a routing page, not a place to explain everything.

Every section ends with a way forward (a card link or a CTA).

**SECTION 5**

**Module Pages Content Plan**

11 pages · template: module

All 11 module pages share one content structure so they feel consistent and are quick to produce. Fill the same blocks with module-specific content.

### **5.1 Module content block pattern**

| **#** | **Block**           | **Content to provide**                                               |
| ----- | ------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 1     | Hero                | Module name, one-line benefit, Request a Demo CTA                    |
| 2     | Overview            | What this module does, in plain language (2-3 sentences)             |
| 3     | Key capabilities    | 4-8 capability blocks (title + one line each)                        |
| 4     | Benefits / outcomes | What the customer gains (time, cost, control, visibility)            |
| 5     | How it fits         | How this module connects to the rest of Marmidon (integration story) |
| 6     | Relevant sectors    | Which of the 12 sectors this matters most for (links)                |
| 7     | Proof               | A quote or mini case relevant to this module                         |
| 8     | Related modules     | 2-4 related module cards                                             |
| 9     | Final CTA band      | Request a Demo + Contact                                             |

### **5.2 Per-module page angle (at-a-glance index)**

The table below is the quick index - the single-line angle each module page should lead with. The full content brief for every module follows in §5.3.

| **Code** | **Module**                         | **Page angle (lead message)**                                                                     |
| -------- | ---------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **M1**   | Financial Management & Accounting  | Control, accuracy and real-time financial visibility - ledgers, payables, receivables, reporting. |
| **M2**   | Human Resources & Payroll          | People and pay handled correctly - records, leave, payroll, compliance, self-service.             |
| **M3**   | Inventory & Warehouse              | Know what you hold and where - stock accuracy, warehouse control, valuation.                      |
| **M4**   | Procurement & Suppliers            | Buy better and control spend - requisitions, suppliers, approvals, purchase orders.               |
| **M5**   | Sales & CRM                        | Win and keep customers - pipeline, quotes, orders, customer history in one place.                 |
| **M6**   | Operations Management              | Run the day-to-day - coordinate operations, workflow and service delivery.                        |
| **M7**   | Fleet Management                   | Control vehicles and costs - fleet records, maintenance, utilisation, fuel.                       |
| **M8**   | Manufacturing & Production         | From order to finished goods - production planning, BOMs, work orders, costing.                   |
| **M9**   | Point of Sale & Retail             | Sell at the counter - fast, reliable POS tied straight into stock and finance.                    |
| **M10**  | Business Intelligence & Dashboards | See the whole business - dashboards and reports across every module.                              |
| **M11**  | Project & Job Costing              | Deliver projects profitably - budgets, job costing, billing and margin.                           |

**NOTE · Reuse the structure, change the detail**

Every module page uses the same nine-block pattern in §5.1. Only the module-specific content changes, so pages are fast to produce and consistent to read.

§5.3 gives the developer and content team the ready-to-refine content for each module page: a draft overview, a full capability list, the outcomes to sell, and the integration and sector links.

**SECTION 5.3**

**Per-Module Capability Detail**

The full content brief for each of the 11 module pages

For each module, this section provides ready-to-refine starting copy for the page overview, a detailed capability list (the source for the "Key capabilities" block), the outcomes to sell, and the module's integration and sector links. Capability names should map to real Marmidon features - confirm and adjust wording with the product team where needed.

### **M1 · Financial Management & Accounting**

_Page URL: /products/financials · Template: module_

**DEVELOPER GUIDANCE · Starting overview copy (refine with Marmidon)**

Marmidon's Financial Management & Accounting module gives you a single, always-current view of your finances. From the general ledger through payables, receivables and reporting, every transaction across Marmidon flows into your accounts automatically - so your numbers are accurate, your close is faster, and you are always audit-ready.

#### **Key capabilities - content for the capabilities block**

| **Capability**                 | **What it does - copy angle**                                                             |
| ------------------------------ | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **General Ledger**             | Chart of accounts, journals and a real-time ledger that is the backbone of your finances. |
| **Accounts Payable**           | Supplier bills, approvals and payments - fed directly from Procurement.                   |
| **Accounts Receivable**        | Customer invoices, receipts and statements - fed directly from Sales and POS.             |
| **Bank & Cash Management**     | Bank accounts, cash books and reconciliation to keep cash under control.                  |
| **Fixed Assets**               | Asset register, depreciation and disposals in line with policy.                           |
| **Budgeting & Commitments**    | Set budgets, track commitments and see variance as it happens.                            |
| **Multi-Currency**             | Transact and report in multiple currencies with revaluation.                              |
| **Tax & Statutory Compliance** | Handle VAT, withholding and statutory reporting accurately.                               |
| **Financial Reporting**        | Trial balance, P&L, balance sheet and cash flow on demand.                                |
| **Period & Year-End Close**    | Structured, controlled close with locking and audit trail.                                |
| **Cost Centres & Dimensions**  | Analyse results by branch, department, project or any dimension.                          |

#### **Outcomes to sell - the benefits block**

- A real-time, accurate financial position at any moment
- A faster, more controlled month- and year-end close
- Fewer manual errors through automatic posting from other modules
- Audit-ready records with a complete trail
- Tighter control of cash, spend and budgets

| **Integrates with**                                                                                                                                                                    | **Most relevant sectors**                                                                                  |
| -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Procurement (payables), Sales & CRM and POS (receivables), Inventory (valuation & cost of sales), HR & Payroll (payroll journals), Projects (job costs), and BI (financial analytics). | Every sector - with particular depth for Manufacturing, Wholesale, Construction, Education and Non-Profit. |

### **M2 · Human Resources & Payroll**

_Page URL: /products/hr-payroll · Template: module_

**DEVELOPER GUIDANCE · Starting overview copy (refine with Marmidon)**

Marmidon's Human Resources & Payroll module keeps your people records and your payroll correct, compliant and in one place. From hire to retire, it manages employee information, leave, benefits and statutory-accurate payroll - and posts straight into your accounts, so HR and Finance always agree.

#### **Key capabilities - content for the capabilities block**

| **Capability**               | **What it does - copy angle**                                              |
| ---------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Employee Records**         | A single, complete record for every employee - personal, job and pay data. |
| **Organisation & Positions** | Structure, positions, grades and reporting lines.                          |
| **Leave & Attendance**       | Leave types, balances, requests, approvals and attendance.                 |
| **Payroll Processing**       | Accurate, repeatable payroll runs with earnings and deductions.            |
| **Statutory Deductions**     | PAYE, NSSF and other statutory items handled correctly.                    |
| **Payslips & Self-Service**  | Digital payslips and employee self-service for requests and details.       |
| **Benefits & Loans**         | Manage allowances, benefits, staff loans and recoveries.                   |
| **Contracts & Documents**    | Employment contracts, documents and expiry reminders.                      |
| **Performance**              | Appraisals and performance tracking against objectives.                    |
| **Recruitment & Onboarding** | Vacancies, applicants and structured onboarding.                           |
| **Payroll GL Posting**       | Automatic posting of payroll costs to the correct accounts.                |

#### **Outcomes to sell - the benefits block**

- Employees paid accurately and on time, every time
- Confidence in statutory and tax compliance
- Less HR and payroll admin through automation and self-service
- A single, reliable source of people data
- HR and Finance permanently in step

| **Integrates with**                                                                                 | **Most relevant sectors**                                                              |
| --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Financials (payroll journals & costs), Projects (labour/time costing), and Operations (resourcing). | Every sector - especially Professional Services, Education, Healthcare and Non-Profit. |

### **M3 · Inventory & Warehouse**

_Page URL: /products/inventory · Template: module_

**DEVELOPER GUIDANCE · Starting overview copy (refine with Marmidon)**

Marmidon's Inventory & Warehouse module tells you exactly what you hold, where it is, and what it is worth - across every store and warehouse. It controls receipts, issues, transfers and counts, and values your stock correctly, so you avoid both stock-outs and dead capital tied up in the wrong items.

#### **Key capabilities - content for the capabilities block**

| **Capability**               | **What it does - copy angle**                                    |
| ---------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Item Master & Categories** | Structured item catalogue with units, categories and attributes. |
| **Multi-Store / Warehouse**  | Track stock across many locations from one system.               |
| **Receipts & Issues**        | Controlled goods-in and goods-out movements.                     |
| **Stock Transfers**          | Move stock between locations with full traceability.             |
| **Counts & Adjustments**     | Stock takes, cycle counts and controlled adjustments.            |
| **Reorder & Replenishment**  | Reorder levels and prompts to prevent stock-outs.                |
| **Batch / Serial / Expiry**  | Track by batch, serial number and expiry where needed.           |
| **Stock Valuation**          | FIFO or weighted-average valuation feeding Finance.              |
| **Barcode Support**          | Barcode-driven receiving, picking and counting.                  |
| **Goods Received Notes**     | GRNs linking purchase orders to stock and payables.              |
| **Inventory Reporting**      | Stock on hand, movement, ageing and valuation reports.           |

#### **Outcomes to sell - the benefits block**

- Accurate stock you can trust
- Fewer stock-outs and less over-ordering
- Reduced shrinkage and write-offs
- Correct stock valuation in the accounts
- Full control across multiple sites

| **Integrates with**                                                                                                                                                   | **Most relevant sectors**                                                   |
| --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Procurement (goods receipt), Sales & CRM and POS (fulfilment & availability), Manufacturing (materials & finished goods), and Financials (valuation & cost of sales). | Wholesale, Retail, Distribution, Manufacturing, Healthcare and Hospitality. |

### **M4 · Procurement & Suppliers**

_Page URL: /products/procurement · Template: module_

**DEVELOPER GUIDANCE · Starting overview copy (refine with Marmidon)**

Marmidon's Procurement & Suppliers module puts your buying under control. Requisitions, approvals, purchase orders and supplier invoices follow one disciplined, auditable flow - with three-way matching - so you buy the right things, at the right price, with a clear record of who approved what.

#### **Key capabilities - content for the capabilities block**

| **Capability**              | **What it does - copy angle**                              |
| --------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Supplier Master**         | Central supplier records, terms, contacts and price lists. |
| **Purchase Requisitions**   | Raise and route internal purchase requests.                |
| **Approval Workflows**      | Configurable, multi-level approval with an audit trail.    |
| **RFQs & Comparison**       | Request and compare supplier quotations.                   |
| **Purchase Orders**         | Generate, issue and track POs to suppliers.                |
| **Goods Receipt**           | Receive against POs, feeding Inventory and matching.       |
| **Invoice Matching**        | Three-way match of PO, receipt and invoice.                |
| **Supplier Payments**       | Prepare and track payments through Finance.                |
| **Contracts & Price Lists** | Agreed pricing and contract terms per supplier.            |
| **Spend Analysis**          | See what is being bought, from whom, and at what cost.     |

#### **Outcomes to sell - the benefits block**

- Controlled, approved spend - no maverick buying
- Better prices and supplier terms
- Accurate three-way matching before payment
- A complete, auditable procurement trail
- Clear visibility of committed and actual spend

| **Integrates with**                                                                                                   | **Most relevant sectors**                                                        |
| --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Inventory (goods receipt), Financials (payables & payments), Projects (project purchasing), and BI (spend analytics). | Manufacturing, Construction, Healthcare, Education, Non-Profit and Distribution. |

### **M5 · Sales & CRM**

_Page URL: /products/sales-crm · Template: module_

**DEVELOPER GUIDANCE · Starting overview copy (refine with Marmidon)**

Marmidon's Sales & CRM module brings your customers, pipeline and orders into one place - from first enquiry to paid invoice. Your team can see every customer's full history, move deals through the pipeline, quote and order fast, and keep receivables under control, all connected to stock and finance.

#### **Key capabilities - content for the capabilities block**

| **Capability**                 | **What it does - copy angle**                          |
| ------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------ |
| **Customer Master & Contacts** | A single view of every customer and contact.           |
| **Leads & Opportunities**      | Capture leads and manage opportunities to close.       |
| **Pipeline Management**        | Track deals through defined sales stages.              |
| **Quotations**                 | Create and send professional quotes quickly.           |
| **Sales Orders**               | Convert quotes to orders linked to stock and delivery. |
| **Pricing & Discounts**        | Price lists, discount rules and approvals.             |
| **Invoicing**                  | Raise invoices that post straight to receivables.      |
| **Statements & Receivables**   | Customer statements, ageing and collections.           |
| **Activity History**           | Every call, email and note against the customer.       |
| **Sales Reporting & Targets**  | Performance against targets and forecasts.             |

#### **Outcomes to sell - the benefits block**

- More deals won through a managed pipeline
- One complete view of every customer
- Faster quote-to-cash
- Better forecasting and target tracking
- Stronger customer retention

| **Integrates with**                                                                                                                       | **Most relevant sectors**                                                      |
| ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| Financials (receivables & invoicing), Inventory (availability), POS (retail sales), Projects (project billing), and BI (sales analytics). | Wholesale, Distribution, Professional Services, Media & Publishing and Retail. |

### **M6 · Operations Management**

_Page URL: /products/operations · Template: module_

**DEVELOPER GUIDANCE · Starting overview copy (refine with Marmidon)**

Marmidon's Operations Management module coordinates the day-to-day running of your business. It organises work, jobs and services into clear, trackable processes - so nothing falls through the cracks, everyone knows what is theirs, and managers can see the state of work at a glance.

#### **Key capabilities - content for the capabilities block**

| **Capability**                | **What it does - copy angle**                         |
| ----------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------- |
| **Workflow & Tasks**          | Define and track tasks through structured workflows.  |
| **Service & Job Scheduling**  | Schedule jobs, services and resources.                |
| **Work Orders**               | Raise and manage work orders end to end.              |
| **Resource Allocation**       | Assign people and resources to the right work.        |
| **SLA & Turnaround Tracking** | Track service levels and turnaround times.            |
| **Document Management**       | Attach and manage operational documents.              |
| **Approvals & Notifications** | Route approvals and alert the right people.           |
| **Operational Dashboards**    | See work in progress and bottlenecks live.            |
| **Checklists & SOPs**         | Standardise processes with checklists and procedures. |

#### **Outcomes to sell - the benefits block**

- Smoother, more predictable day-to-day operations
- Clear visibility of work in progress
- Fewer bottlenecks and missed handovers
- Accountability for every task
- Consistent, repeatable processes

| **Integrates with**                                            | **Most relevant sectors**                                                    |
| -------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Projects, HR & Payroll (resourcing), Fleet, Financials and BI. | Production, Distribution, Professional Services, Healthcare and Hospitality. |

### **M7 · Fleet Management**

_Page URL: /products/fleet · Template: module_

**DEVELOPER GUIDANCE · Starting overview copy (refine with Marmidon)**

Marmidon's Fleet Management module keeps your vehicles running, compliant and cost-controlled. It records every vehicle and driver, schedules maintenance, tracks fuel and utilisation, and flags licence and insurance renewals - so you cut running costs and downtime while staying on the right side of compliance.

#### **Key capabilities - content for the capabilities block**

| **Capability**                | **What it does - copy angle**                          |
| ----------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------ |
| **Vehicle Register**          | Complete record of every vehicle and its history.      |
| **Driver Management**         | Drivers, licences and assignments.                     |
| **Maintenance Scheduling**    | Planned and preventive maintenance by time or mileage. |
| **Fuel Management**           | Fuel issues, consumption and cost per vehicle.         |
| **Trip & Mileage Logs**       | Trips, mileage and route records.                      |
| **Insurance & Licences**      | Renewal tracking and reminders to avoid lapses.        |
| **Spare Parts & Workshop**    | Workshop jobs and spare-parts usage.                   |
| **Utilisation & Cost per km** | Utilisation and true running cost per vehicle.         |
| **Incident Logging**          | Record and follow up incidents and repairs.            |

#### **Outcomes to sell - the benefits block**

- Lower fleet running costs
- Less unplanned downtime
- No missed licence or insurance renewals
- Clear utilisation and cost-per-kilometre visibility
- A safer, better-managed fleet

| **Integrates with**                                                                    | **Most relevant sectors**                                             |
| -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Procurement (parts & fuel), Inventory (spares), Financials (costs), Operations and BI. | Distribution, Construction, Manufacturing, Non-Profit and Healthcare. |

### **M8 · Manufacturing & Production**

_Page URL: /products/manufacturing · Template: module_

**DEVELOPER GUIDANCE · Starting overview copy (refine with Marmidon)**

Marmidon's Manufacturing & Production module takes you from order to finished goods with control over materials, work and cost. Bills of materials, routings, work orders and production costing come together so you plan capacity, use materials efficiently, and know the true cost of everything you make.

#### **Key capabilities - content for the capabilities block**

| **Capability**              | **What it does - copy angle**                        |
| --------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------- |
| **Bills of Materials**      | Define what goes into every product, at every level. |
| **Routings & Work Centres** | Production steps, work centres and capacity.         |
| **Production Planning**     | Plan production against demand and capacity.         |
| **Work Orders**             | Issue and track work orders on the shop floor.       |
| **Material Requirements**   | Calculate material needs from orders and BOMs.       |
| **Shop-Floor Tracking**     | Track progress, output and consumption.              |
| **Production Costing**      | Capture material, labour and overhead cost per job.  |
| **Quality Checks**          | Quality control points within production.            |
| **By-Products & Scrap**     | Account for by-products, scrap and yield.            |
| **Capacity View**           | See load against available capacity.                 |

#### **Outcomes to sell - the benefits block**

- Production delivered on time
- Accurate, true product costing
- Less material waste
- Capacity planned, not guessed
- Full traceability from material to finished good

| **Integrates with**                                                                                                 | **Most relevant sectors**                                        |
| ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Inventory (materials & finished goods), Procurement (material supply), Projects, Financials (WIP & costing) and BI. | Manufacturing and Production - the core sectors for this module. |

### **M9 · Point of Sale & Retail**

_Page URL: /products/pos-retail · Template: module_

**DEVELOPER GUIDANCE · Starting overview copy (refine with Marmidon)**

Marmidon's Point of Sale & Retail module lets you sell fast and reliably at the counter, with every sale flowing straight into stock and finance. Built for real shop and outlet conditions - multiple outlets, multiple payment methods, quick returns and clean cash-ups - it keeps your stock and takings accurate in real time.

#### **Key capabilities - content for the capabilities block**

| **Capability**                     | **What it does - copy angle**                        |
| ---------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------- |
| **Touch-Friendly POS**             | Fast, simple point-of-sale screen for busy counters. |
| **Fast Checkout & Scanning**       | Barcode scanning and rapid checkout.                 |
| **Multiple Payment Methods**       | Cash, card, mobile money and split payments.         |
| **Discounts & Promotions**         | Apply promotions and discounts at the till.          |
| **Returns & Exchanges**            | Handle returns and exchanges cleanly.                |
| **Cash-Up & Shift Reconciliation** | Reconcile tills, shifts and floats.                  |
| **Multi-Outlet**                   | Run and compare multiple outlets centrally.          |
| **Offline Resilience**             | Keep selling if connectivity drops.                  |
| **Loyalty (optional)**             | Recognise and reward repeat customers.               |
| **Real-Time Posting**              | Sales and stock updated live in the system.          |

#### **Outcomes to sell - the benefits block**

- Fast, reliable selling at the counter
- Accurate daily takings and cash control
- Live stock across every outlet
- Central control of multiple shops
- A smoother experience for customers

| **Integrates with**                                                                     | **Most relevant sectors**          |
| --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------- |
| Inventory (real-time stock), Financials (sales & cash), Sales & CRM (customers) and BI. | Retail, Hospitality and Wholesale. |

### **M10 · Business Intelligence & Dashboards**

_Page URL: /products/business-intelligence · Template: module_

**DEVELOPER GUIDANCE · Starting overview copy (refine with Marmidon)**

Marmidon's Business Intelligence & Dashboards module turns the data from every module into decisions. Executive dashboards, KPIs and drill-down reports give leaders one version of the truth across finance, sales, stock, people and operations - live, not weeks later in a spreadsheet.

#### **Key capabilities - content for the capabilities block**

| **Capability**                  | **What it does - copy angle**                          |
| ------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------ |
| **Executive Dashboards**        | At-a-glance dashboards for leaders.                    |
| **Cross-Module Reporting**      | Report across finance, sales, stock and more together. |
| **KPIs & Scorecards**           | Track the metrics that matter with targets.            |
| **Drill-Down Analysis**         | Move from summary to transaction in a click.           |
| **Financial Analytics**         | Analyse performance, margin and cost.                  |
| **Sales & Inventory Analytics** | See what sells, what moves and what does not.          |
| **Custom Reports**              | Build the reports your business needs.                 |
| **Scheduled Delivery**          | Send reports automatically to the right people.        |
| **Data Visualisation**          | Charts and visuals that make data clear.               |
| **Role-Based Views**            | Show each role the numbers relevant to them.           |

#### **Outcomes to sell - the benefits block**

- Decisions made from live, trusted data
- One version of the truth across the business
- Early warning when a KPI moves the wrong way
- Far less manual reporting effort
- Visibility from the shop floor to the board

| **Integrates with**                                                                    | **Most relevant sectors**                                                                 |
| -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Every Marmidon module - BI is the reporting and analytics layer over all of your data. | Every sector - especially data-intensive Manufacturing, Wholesale and Media & Publishing. |

### **M11 · Project & Job Costing**

_Page URL: /products/projects · Template: module_

**DEVELOPER GUIDANCE · Starting overview copy (refine with Marmidon)**

Marmidon's Project & Job Costing module makes sure every project and job is delivered to budget and to margin. Budgets, time, materials and purchasing are tracked against each job, with progress billing and live profitability - so you always know whether the work is making or losing money.

#### **Key capabilities - content for the capabilities block**

| **Capability**                     | **What it does - copy angle**                  |
| ---------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------- |
| **Project & Job Setup**            | Define projects, jobs, phases and structures.  |
| **Budgets & Estimates**            | Set budgets and estimates to cost against.     |
| **Time & Labour Capture**          | Capture labour and time to each job.           |
| **Project Purchasing & Materials** | Buy and allocate materials to projects.        |
| **Cost Tracking**                  | Track actual cost against budget in real time. |
| **Milestones & Billing**           | Bill by milestone, stage or schedule.          |
| **Progress & Retention Billing**   | Handle progress claims and retentions.         |
| **Profitability & Margin**         | See margin and profitability per job.          |
| **Resource Assignment**            | Assign people and resources to projects.       |
| **Project Reporting**              | Report on status, cost, billing and margin.    |

#### **Outcomes to sell - the benefits block**

- Projects delivered to budget
- Live visibility of margin and profitability
- Accurate, timely project billing
- Controlled project spend
- Profit understood job by job

| **Integrates with**                                                                                              | **Most relevant sectors**                                                           |
| ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Financials (costs & billing), Procurement (project buying), HR & Payroll (labour), Inventory (materials) and BI. | Construction, Professional Services, Media & Publishing, Non-Profit and Production. |

**BEST PRACTICE · Consistency is the point**

Each module page above follows the identical structure from §5.1, so the eleven pages read as one family.

The "integrates with" links are what let you cross-sell - every module page should point the reader to the connected modules and the sectors where it matters most.

**SECTION 6**

**Sector Pages Content Plan**

12 pages · template: sector

Sector pages speak the language of each industry and show which modules solve that industry's problems. They are key landing pages for industry-specific search.

### **6.1 Sector content block pattern**

| **#** | **Block**           | **Content to provide**                                            |
| ----- | ------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 1     | Hero                | "Marmidon for \[sector\]", one-line fit statement, Request a Demo |
| 2     | Sector challenges   | 3-5 pain points this industry recognises                          |
| 3     | How Marmidon helps  | How the platform answers those challenges                         |
| 4     | Recommended modules | The modules most relevant to this sector (cards/links)            |
| 5     | Sector proof        | A relevant case study or testimonial                              |
| 6     | Final CTA band      | Request a Demo + Contact                                          |

### **6.2 Per-sector content brief**

| **Sector**            | **Recommended module emphasis (content angle)**                          |
| --------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| Manufacturing         | Manufacturing & Production, Inventory, Procurement, Financials, BI       |
| Production            | Manufacturing & Production, Operations, Inventory, Project & Job Costing |
| Wholesale             | Inventory & Warehouse, Sales & CRM, Procurement, Financials              |
| Retail                | POS & Retail, Inventory, Sales & CRM, Financials                         |
| Distribution          | Inventory & Warehouse, Fleet, Sales & CRM, Operations                    |
| Professional Services | Project & Job Costing, HR & Payroll, Financials, CRM                     |
| Education             | Financials, HR & Payroll, Procurement, Operations                        |
| Healthcare            | Inventory, Procurement, HR & Payroll, Financials                         |
| Hospitality           | POS & Retail, Inventory, HR & Payroll, Financials                        |
| Non-Profit            | Financials, HR & Payroll, Procurement, Project & Job Costing             |
| Construction          | Project & Job Costing, Procurement, Fleet, Financials                    |
| Media & Publishing    | Project & Job Costing, Sales & CRM, Financials, BI                       |

**DEVELOPER GUIDANCE · Write to the industry, not the software**

Open each sector page with the industry's own language and problems - then introduce Marmidon as the answer.

The recommended-module emphasis above must stay consistent with the Module × Sector matrix in SIA-003 Appendix A.

**SECTION 7**

**Customers, Pricing, About, Contact & Resources**

### **7.1 Customers & Case Studies**

| **Block**         | **Content**                                                                                            |
| ----------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| Customers hub     | Intro line, filterable grid of case-study cards (by sector/module)                                     |
| Case study (each) | Client + sector, challenge, Marmidon solution (modules used), measurable outcomes, customer quote, CTA |

**BEST PRACTICE · Case studies are the strongest sales asset**

Enterprise buyers trust peer proof. Each case study should name the sector, the modules used and at least one concrete outcome.

Keep a consistent structure: Challenge → Solution → Outcome → Quote.

### **7.2 Pricing**

| **Block**         | **Content**                                                         |
| ----------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Intro             | How Marmidon is licensed/priced (or "how pricing works" if bespoke) |
| Tiers or approach | Editions/packages or a tailored-quote explanation                   |
| What's included   | What every customer gets (support, updates, onboarding)             |
| FAQ               | Common pricing questions (eligible for FAQ schema, TSR §6.3)        |
| CTA               | Request a Demo / Request a Quote                                    |

**NOTE · Pricing approach is a Marmidon decision**

The site must have a pricing page (benchmarked vendors all do). Whether it shows figures or a "request a quote" model is Marmidon's commercial call - the template supports either.

### **7.3 About / Company**

| **Block**               | **Content**                                                         |
| ----------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Company story           | Who Marmidon is, mission, what it builds                            |
| What we do              | Develop, license, support and maintain Marmidon Enterprise Software |
| Leadership/team         | Optional - key people with photo, role, short bio                   |
| Why customers choose us | Credibility, longevity, sector breadth, support                     |
| CTA                     | Contact / Request a Demo                                            |

### **7.4 Contact**

| **Block**    | **Content**                                              |
| ------------ | -------------------------------------------------------- |
| Contact form | Name, company, email, sector, message + consent (TSR §8) |
| Demo path    | Clear link/option to request a demo specifically         |
| Details      | Email, phone, address, hours                             |
| Map          | Office location (lazy-loaded, consent-aware)             |

### **7.5 Resources & Articles**

| **Block**      | **Content**                                                   |
| -------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Resources hub  | Intro, filter/category, grid of resource cards                |
| Article/blog   | Title, author, date, body, related links, CTA; Article schema |
| Guides (gated) | Whitepaper landing with lead form (resource_download event)   |

**SECTION 8**

**Per-Page SEO Metadata**

Title, meta description & H1 for every page

Provide a unique title, meta description and H1 for each page per TSR-004 §6.2. The table below gives ready-to-refine starting metadata; the content team finalises wording with Marmidon. {Module} / {Sector} placeholders are replaced per page.

### **8.1 Core & hub pages**

| **Page**      | **Title (≤ 60 chars)**                           | **H1**                             |
| ------------- | ------------------------------------------------ | ---------------------------------- |
| Home          | Marmidon Enterprise Software \| Integrated ERP   | Run your whole business on one ERP |
| Products hub  | ERP Modules \| Marmidon Enterprise Software      | Explore the Marmidon modules       |
| Solutions hub | ERP by Industry \| Marmidon Enterprise Software  | Marmidon for your industry         |
| Customers     | Customer Stories \| Marmidon Enterprise Software | Customer success with Marmidon     |
| Pricing       | Pricing \| Marmidon Enterprise Software          | Marmidon pricing                   |
| Partners      | Partners \| Marmidon Enterprise Software         | Partner with Marmidon              |
| About         | About Marmidon \| Enterprise Software            | About Marmidon                     |
| Contact       | Contact Marmidon \| Request a Demo               | Talk to Marmidon                   |
| Resources     | Resources & Insights \| Marmidon                 | Resources & insights               |

### **8.2 Module pages**

| **Module**                  | **Title**                                  | **H1**                     |
| --------------------------- | ------------------------------------------ | -------------------------- |
| Financial Management & Acc… | Financial & Accounting \| Marmidon ERP     | Financial & Accounting     |
| Human Resources & Payroll   | Human Resources & Payroll \| Marmidon ERP  | Human Resources & Payroll  |
| Inventory & Warehouse       | Inventory & Warehouse \| Marmidon ERP      | Inventory & Warehouse      |
| Procurement & Suppliers     | Procurement & Suppliers \| Marmidon ERP    | Procurement & Suppliers    |
| Sales & CRM                 | Sales & CRM \| Marmidon ERP                | Sales & CRM                |
| Operations Management       | Operations \| Marmidon ERP                 | Operations                 |
| Fleet Management            | Fleet \| Marmidon ERP                      | Fleet                      |
| Manufacturing & Production  | Manufacturing & Production \| Marmidon ERP | Manufacturing & Production |
| Point of Sale & Retail      | Point of Sale & Retail \| Marmidon ERP     | Point of Sale & Retail     |
| Business Intelligence & Da… | Business Intelligence \| Marmidon ERP      | Business Intelligence      |
| Project & Job Costing       | Project & Job Costing \| Marmidon ERP      | Project & Job Costing      |

### **8.3 Sector pages**

| **Sector**            | **Title**                                 | **H1**                             |
| --------------------- | ----------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------- |
| Manufacturing         | ERP for Manufacturing \| Marmidon         | Marmidon for Manufacturing         |
| Production            | ERP for Production \| Marmidon            | Marmidon for Production            |
| Wholesale             | ERP for Wholesale \| Marmidon             | Marmidon for Wholesale             |
| Retail                | ERP for Retail \| Marmidon                | Marmidon for Retail                |
| Distribution          | ERP for Distribution \| Marmidon          | Marmidon for Distribution          |
| Professional Services | ERP for Professional Services \| Marmidon | Marmidon for Professional Services |
| Education             | ERP for Education \| Marmidon             | Marmidon for Education             |
| Healthcare            | ERP for Healthcare \| Marmidon            | Marmidon for Healthcare            |
| Hospitality           | ERP for Hospitality \| Marmidon           | Marmidon for Hospitality           |
| Non-Profit            | ERP for Non-Profit \| Marmidon            | Marmidon for Non-Profit            |
| Construction          | ERP for Construction \| Marmidon          | Marmidon for Construction          |
| Media & Publishing    | ERP for Media & Publishing \| Marmidon    | Marmidon for Media & Publishing    |

**SPECIFICATION · Metadata rules**

Every title unique and ≤ 60 characters; every meta description unique, 140-160 characters, benefit-led.

Exactly one H1 per page; H1 should match the user's intent for that page.

Finalise all metadata before launch - it is part of the SEO checklist in TSR-004 §12.2.

**SECTION 9**

**Call-to-Action Matrix**

One primary action per page

Every page has a clear primary action. Secondary actions support it but never compete with it.

| **Page / template** | **Primary CTA**        | **Secondary CTA**            |
| ------------------- | ---------------------- | ---------------------------- |
| Home                | Request a Demo         | Explore modules / industries |
| Module              | Request a Demo         | See relevant industries      |
| Sector              | Request a Demo         | View recommended modules     |
| Customers / Case    | Request a Demo         | Read another story           |
| Pricing             | Request a Demo / Quote | Contact sales                |
| Resources / Article | Request a Demo         | Read related / subscribe     |
| About               | Contact us             | Request a Demo               |
| Contact             | Submit enquiry         | Request a Demo               |
| Partners            | Become a partner       | Contact us                   |

**MUST-HAVE · Demo is the conversion**

Per PRD, the demo request is the primary conversion goal. Request a Demo must be present and prominent on every commercial page and in the global header.

**SECTION 10**

**Content Asset Checklist**

Content is more than words. The following assets must be gathered, branded and optimised (TSR-004 §4.3) before pages are complete.

| **Asset**           | **For**                      | **Status** |
| ------------------- | ---------------------------- | ---------- |
| Logo (SVG + PNG)    | Header, footer, share images | PROVIDED   |
| Module icons (11)   | Module cards & pages         | TO SOURCE  |
| Sector imagery (12) | Sector heroes                | TO SOURCE  |
| Hero imagery        | Homepage & hubs              | TO SOURCE  |
| Customer logos      | Proof strips                 | TO CONFIRM |
| Case study content  | Customers section            | TO GATHER  |
| Team photos         | About page                   | OPTIONAL   |
| Product screenshots | Module pages                 | TO GATHER  |
| Social share images | Open Graph (TSR §6.2)        | TO CREATE  |
| Favicon / app icons | Browser & devices            | TO CREATE  |

**SECTION 11**

**Content Readiness Tracker**

What's ready, what's outstanding

A single view of content status across the site, so the programme can see what blocks launch. Update as copy is written and approved.

| **Page group**       | **Pages**   | **Owner**                   | **Status** |
| -------------------- | ----------- | --------------------------- | ---------- |
| Homepage             | 1           | Marmidon + content          | TO WRITE   |
| Products hub         | 1           | Content                     | TO WRITE   |
| Module pages         | 11          | Content (templated)         | TO WRITE   |
| Solutions hub        | 1           | Content                     | TO WRITE   |
| Sector pages         | 12          | Content (templated)         | TO WRITE   |
| Customers + cases    | 1 + cases   | Marmidon (source) + content | TO GATHER  |
| Pricing              | 1           | Marmidon (commercial)       | TO WRITE   |
| About / Company      | 1           | Marmidon + content          | TO WRITE   |
| Contact              | 1           | Content + dev               | TO WRITE   |
| Partners             | 1           | Marmidon + content          | TO WRITE   |
| Resources + articles | hub + posts | Content / marketing         | TO WRITE   |
| Legal pages          | 3-4         | Marmidon (adviser) + dev    | TO WRITE   |

**BEST PRACTICE · Launch with quality, fill depth over time**

Prioritise final copy for the homepage, the 11 module pages, the top sector pages and the demo/contact flow - these carry conversion.

Resources and additional case studies can grow after launch without holding back go-live.

**SECTION 12**

**Content Workflow & Governance**

| **Step** | **Activity**                                     | **Owner**           |
| -------- | ------------------------------------------------ | ------------------- |
| 1        | Draft copy from the block plans in this document | Content team        |
| 2        | Marmidon review for accuracy & voice (§2)        | Marmidon            |
| 3        | Approve & mark FINAL in the tracker (§11)        | Marmidon            |
| 4        | Load into CMS structured fields (TSR §2.3)       | Developer / content |
| 5        | Apply per-page SEO metadata (§8)                 | SEO / content       |
| 6        | Proofread on staging in real templates           | Content + Marmidon  |
| 7        | Sign off as part of UAT (PRD §12)                | Marmidon            |

**NOTE · End of content plan**

With this document, the structure (SIA-003), the design (WDD-002) and the engineering (TSR-004) all have their content counterpart - completing the brief for the Marmidon website build.

**- End of Document MARM-WEB-CPM-005 -**
